title: push uploads already existing layers
body:
### Issue Description

As suggested by @vrothberg in https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/10247#issuecomment-1419024369 reopening the same issue as this becomes a daily business issue, we can reproduce it, and `skopeo` does not have this issue.

Our images have base layers of several GB size. The CI produces daily diffs as a new, tiny layer. However, Podman (and Buidah) are unable to detect the existance of the large base layers in the registry, and perform a re-upload again in every CI step. This produces a significant amount of traffic and delays of the pipeline.

This happens, because our CI jobs do not have a warm graphroot cache and will never since they are ephemeral pods.

**Skopeo** however does not have this bug - see the below.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue

**Podman or Buildah**. 

```sh
for i in {1..2}; do 
podman run -it --rm --user podman podman:latest sh -c "\
  podman pull docker.io/library/mysql:5.7.41 && \
  podman tag docker.io/library/mysql:5.7.41 ttl.sh/testimg:1h && \
  time podman push ttl.sh/testimg:1h"
done
```

However run the same with **Skopeo**:

```sh
for i in {1..2}; do
  time podman run -it --rm --user skopeo skopeo:latest copy docker://docker.io/library/mysql:5.7.41 docker://ttl.sh/testimg:1h
done
```

### Describe the results you received

One would expect, that the second time Podman will discover the existance of the layers upstream, but it does not and uploads every layer again.

Podman or Buildah:
```
Copying blob 2ec521688c56 [===>----------------------------------] 4.7MiB / 48.1MiB
Copying blob 3e6a5b12e1de [================>---------------------] 11.0MiB / 24.4MiB
Copying blob c9bd281f4600 [=========>----------------------------] 12.0MiB / 46.2MiB
...
```

Skopeo:
```
Copying blob xxx skipped: **already exists**
Copying blob xxx skipped: **already exists**
...
```

### Describe the results you expected

Like Skopeo, Podman and Buildah should be able to detect the upstream layer exists and skip the upload

### podman info output

comments.body: A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
I think https://github.com/containers/image/pull/1645 should resolve this, but its on a low priority. I'll get back to it.
@flouthoc https://github.com/containers/image/pull/1645 is merged and I'm still seeing this issue with podman 5

> [@flouthoc](https://github.com/flouthoc) [containers/image#1645](https://github.com/containers/image/pull/1645) is merged and I'm still seeing this issue with podman 5

Hi Could you please share a reproducer, as far as I remember we merged a test for this in CI. If you can share a small reproducer we can test if any bug went through.

Test which was added to buildah: https://github.com/containers/buildah/pull/5153/files

Sorry for the late reply

Let me explain what I'm doing. I'm using aws ecr and running rootful podman in CI
On first build podman builds the image using dockerfile, and pushes layers (amd image) to aws ecr, everything works as expected
If I re-ran the same build again on different CI agent, podman again pushes all layers to aws ecr.

This is problematic as image size is too big. In docker, I didn't face any problem, docker is able to detect the image is upload to aws. I thought it may be because podman compresses layers before pushing but I added `--disable-compression=false` to enable compression during build and got the same result


However, if I share the storage among builds (`/var/lib/containers/` folder), podman detects the layers are present in aws ecr and doesn't push any layer to ecr. (May be because of `blob-info-cache-v1.sqlite` is shared between builds)

I also use `--format=docker` while building the container image.
